plan_adherence_notes: All 7 planned steps completed. Helper function defined with correct signature, placement, and error contract. Status code changed 409→422, details dict trimmed to 5 asset_preview-only fields, boundary condition preserved (strict < 0). One justified deviation: call site uses try/except to clean up orphaned command from COMMANDS dict before re-raising ApiError (plan said '1 line added' but 4-line try/except is necessary since ApiError bypasses terminal rejection persistence). Test updates comprehensive across all 4 test classes (unit, property, integration, invariant) — 12 tests modified total. All 290 tests pass.
missed_questions: What happens to the already-created command in COMMANDS when ApiError is raised? The command is inserted into COMMANDS before the min_asset check runs. Since ApiError bypasses build_terminal_rejection_response (which persists the command), the orphaned command entry needs explicit cleanup. This was handled correctly in implementation but was not anticipated in questions or plan.
reason: Implementation correctly extracts min_asset_check as a reusable guard function with ApiError(422) semantics, replacing the old build_terminal_rejection_response(409) flow. The semantic shift from terminal rejection (persisted event, idempotency replay) to transient validation error (no event, retries re-evaluate) is properly reflected in both server.py and all 12 affected tests. The command cleanup pattern is a correct adaptation not in the original plan. All 290 tests pass with zero failures.
